name: Sierra Nevada National Park – Alhama River and Camarate Dehesa (PR-A 33)
name_original: Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada – Río Alhama y Dehesa de Camarate (PR-A 33)
region_intro: Hiking around Lugros offers access to diverse landscapes within the Sierra Nevada National Park and the Granada Geopark. The region features a mix of river valleys, elevated viewpoints, and ravines. Hikers can explore areas characterized by dehesa woodlands and varied geological formations. The terrain provides options ranging from gentle paths to more challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Lugros The most popular hiking route is Sierra Nevada National Park – Alhama River and Camarate Dehesa (PR-A 33), a 11.8 miles (18.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural environment of the Sierra Nevada, following a river and passing through dehesa. Another top favourite among local hikers is End of the World Viewpoint – View of Hoya de Guadix loop from Beas de Guadix, an easy 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This circular route offers panoramic views of the Hoya de Guadix from a notable viewpoint. Local hikers also love the La Tizná Recreation Area – Alcázar Ravine loop from Jérez del Marquesado, a 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through a ravine and recreation area, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Lugros is defined by mountain landscapes, river valleys, and geological formations, with a network of trails offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Lugros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lugros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lugros?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Lugros, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1000 times, contributing to their high average rating.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect when hiking near Lugros?
A: Hiking around Lugros offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Nevada National Park and the Granada Geopark. You'll encounter river valleys, elevated viewpoints, and ravines. The terrain features dehesa woodlands and varied geological formations, providing options from gentle paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lugros offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21833775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the World Viewpoint – View of Hoya de Guadix loop from Beas de Guadix&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.9 km) path that provides panoramic views and is suitable for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Lugros?
A: Yes, many trails in the Lugros area are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21833775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the World Viewpoint – View of Hoya de Guadix loop from Beas de Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of the Hoya de Guadix. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tizná Recreation Area – Alcázar Ravine loop from Jérez del Marquesado&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.4-mile (13.6 km) trail.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in the Lugros area?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21833775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the World Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34349462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark – End of the World Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2.5 hours. More difficult routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985013803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Nevada National Park – Alhama River and Camarate Dehesa (PR-A 33)&lt;/a&gt;, can take nearly 6 hours.

Q: What are some notable natural or historical attractions to see while hiking?
A: Around Lugros, you can explore various attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadix Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6590435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theatre of Guadix&lt;/a&gt; offer historical insights. For natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Alguacil&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7096945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Pelado 3,182 m&lt;/a&gt; within the Sierra Nevada.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Lugros?
A: The best time to hike in Lugros is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant with wildflowers or autumn colors. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings snow to the higher Sierra Nevada peaks.

Q: Are there options for winter hiking in Lugros?
A: Yes, winter hiking is possible, particularly on lower-elevation trails. However, the higher peaks of the Sierra Nevada National Park will be covered in snow and require specialized equipment and experience. Always check weather conditions and trail accessibility before heading out in winter.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Lugros?
A: The trails in Lugros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse mountain landscapes, the serene river valleys, and the unique geological formations found within the Sierra Nevada National Park and the Granada Geopark.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Granada Geopark?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34349462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark – End of the World Viewpoint loop from Beas de Guadix&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.7-mile (7.5 km) trail specifically designed to showcase the unique geological features and panoramic vistas of the Geopark.

Q: What about parking for trailheads in Lugros?
A: Many trailheads in and around Lugros, especially those leading into the Sierra Nevada National Park or near popular viewpoints, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking availability at your chosen starting point.
tips: The Alhama River path is an official PR that takes us along the riverbed until it joins the Dehesa Camarate path. Here we enter the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, which serves as a transition zone to the National Park.

This trail leads to the Dehesa de Camarate, also known as the "Enchanted Forest". It is not difficult to imagine why, since it gives the feeling that you are going to come across a gnome at any moment. This section of the trail is already within the scope of the National Park. The variety of tree species (oak, wild cherry, maple, ash, whitebeam, willow, and gall-oak) makes this an especially scenic spot in the fall, but it's worth visiting in all four seasons to see their changes.
In autumn access is restricted on weekends. The path is very closed in the last part of the route, to avoid this it can be made linear, returning by the same path when arriving at the temptation.
